title: kopsia ramosa subsp. mutelii
intro: Kopsia ramosa subsp. mutelii is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the mutel's kopsia, a type of evergreen shrub native to China and Southeast Asia. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, including antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits associated with Kopsia ramosa subsp. mutelii include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat various infections, as well as its use in traditional remedies for skin conditions and respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems are often used to make teas, infusions, or dried extracts, which are consumed to harness its medicinal and therapeutic properties.
